speaker: Narrator
text: Christina rushes out of the building. Taking a moment to glance back at the register girl, she can see her smiling at her again, but this time her smile feels off.

speaker: Narrator
text: She eventually makes it back home and decided to eat her lunch in the living room.

speaker: Narrator
text: She takes out her food from the bag. A burger, fries, some sauces, and a soda. The food was still hot, and salted to perfection.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina starts with the sandwich, pulling it. up to her mouth opening, and shoving it in. Chewing it vigorously. This had been the most substantial thing she'd eaten in a week. 
	
	She loved the taste of every bite. But then, when she attempted to swallow, she gagged.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Wash it down with something.

speaker: Pasaya
text: How can you expect a girl to take all that at once?

speaker: Christina
text: Yes, Pasaya.

speaker: Narrator
text: She does as she's commanded. Taking about half her drink in her to wash down a chunk of mashed hamburger.

speaker: Christina
text: Is this enough for you?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Hmmm.

speaker: Narrator
text: Something scrambles up Christina's esophagus. Its small hands tickle Christina on their way up to her mouth.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Swallow~

speaker: Christina
text: Yes, Pasaya.

speaker: Narrator
text: Christina gags on the food, she throws up a little in her mouth from how much food she just stuffed into her mouth.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Hmmm.

speaker: Pasaya
text: It's still too much, Christina.

speaker: Christina
text: What should I do?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Have you ever watched a fly eat?

speaker: Pasaya
text: C'mon, I know you've all been that bored before.

speaker: Pasaya
text: The way that they-

speaker: Christina
text: I can't.

speaker: Christina
text: Pasaya, my saliva isn't acidic enough to do something that.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Chew as much of the food as you can and spit it in the cup.

speaker: Christina
text: You have an idea?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Chew now, drone.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina huddles over her tray of food. She stuffs as much of everything on her tray into her mouth at a time and masticates. 
	
	Her cheeks are engorged to the point where it takes as much effort to keep herself from choking as it takes to break down the mush. 
	
	She draws her head over the oversized cup of cola and spews.

speaker: Christina
text: Is that enough?

speaker: Pasaya
text: Stop when I tell you to stop, drone.

speaker: Narrator
text:
Christina continues to gorge herself. Holding on as the tingling sensation runs down her throat into her stomach. 
	
	Something inside her is dangling inside, rubbing up against her inner lining as it wiggles.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Spit.

speaker: Narrator
text: She releases. The cup is nearly filled with regurgitated food. Christina feels the wriggling ascend up from her stomach through her esophagus, into her mouth. She can hardly stand to breathe.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Close your mouth, the taste might be more bitter than you can handle.

speaker: Christina
text: I'll hold it in.

speaker: Christina
text: I swear.

speaker: Narrator
text:
That distinctive taste of peach candy has been mixed with the bitterness of her own stomach acid. The flavor is hard enough to stand, but the burning, the searing pain fills her mouth. 
	
	Sloshing around her gums, dropping on her tongue.

speaker: Pasaya
text: I'll help a little too.

speaker: Narrator
text: A soft spray of viscous goop hits the back of her teeth.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Gargle.

speaker: Pasaya
text: Let it out slowly.

speaker: Christina
text: Ah~.

speaker: Narrator
text: The liquid drips slowly from her tongue onto the damp mass of food in her cup. Swirling the trail of saliva around as it seeps deeper.

speaker: Pasaya
text: You’ve served me perfectly,  precious drone.
